info.name: log-monitoring-service (P8.2a cross-phase closer)
info.description: Postman v2.1 collection for log-monitoring-service after P8.0/P8.1/P8.2 + P8.2a cross-phase closer on :8098. log-monitoring-service hosts the cross-service health aggregation surface (ServiceHealthProbe SPI -- noop / eureka-actuator backends) AND the SLO budget engine SPI (SloBudgetEngine -- noop / micrometer-derivation backends) AND the SloEvaluator @Scheduled orchestrator. P8.2a smoke-stack (scripts/smoke-p8-2a.ps1) boots the service against a real Prometheus 2.55.1 container on :9090 with both binder gates flipped on (cortex.monitoring.probe.backend=eureka-actuator + cortex.monitoring.slo.enabled=true + cortex.monitoring.slo.backend=micrometer-derivation). This collection asserts the actuator surface (LD106 + LD112 bootstrap-registered counter + gauge families), the MonitoringHealthIndicator binding (P8.0 / ADR-0044 D4), the SLO multi-window burn-rate rule file load (P8.2 / ADR-0046 D6) on the Prometheus side, and the Prometheus scrape reachability of the host JVM. Folder order: Admin (actuator health + monitoring health indicator); Metrics-Baseline (snapshot probe_total per outcome); Prometheus-Exposition (assert HELP + TYPE + tag-allowlist for probe counter + both SLO gauges per Part 17); Prometheus-Container (assert Prometheus is healthy + rules parsed + scrape target healthy -- skip if no prometheus_base_url); Metrics-After (re-snapshot to prove counters monotonic). Run AFTER scripts/smoke-p8-2a.ps1 -KeepInfra so the service + Prometheus are both still up.

exec: pm.test('response under 5000 ms (actuator + Prometheus tolerated)', function () {

item.name: Admin (actuator)
item.description: Direct actuator surface exposed by log-monitoring-service on :8098 per application.yml (management.endpoints.web.exposure.include: health,info,metrics,prometheus,beans). The /actuator/health/monitoring indicator (MonitoringHealthIndicator bound to slug 'monitoring' per @Component('monitoring')) is the binder-gate proof at the HTTP boundary (P8.0 / ADR-0044 D4): when cortex.monitoring.probe.backend=eureka-actuator the details.backend value flips from 'noop' to 'eureka-actuator'.

exec: pm.test('details.backend == eureka-actuator (proves binder gate flipped)', function () { pm.expect(body.details && body.details.backend).to.equal('eureka-actuator'); });
pm.test('body parses as JSON object', function () { pm.expect(pm.response.json()).to.be.an('object'); });
pm.test('names is a non-empty array', function () { pm.expect(body.names).to.be.an('array').that.is.not.empty; });
pm.test('cortex.monitoring.probe_total registered (P8.0 OCP bootstrap LD106)', function () { pm.expect(body.names).to.include('cortex.monitoring.probe_total'); });

item.name: Metrics-Baseline
item.description: Snapshot the cortex_monitoring_probe_total counter family BEFORE any further probe ticks. Used to compute deltas in the Metrics-After folder. Sum across all outcomes so the baseline is robust regardless of which outcome the next tick lands in.

item.name: GET /actuator/prometheus baseline -> capture probe counter sum

exec: const re = new RegExp('^' + metricName + '\\{[^}]*backend="' + backend + '"[^}]*\\}\\s+([0-9.eE+-]+)$', 'gm');
// Note whether the SLO gauges are already present at baseline (they appear lazily after the first SloEvaluator tick).

item.name: Prometheus-Exposition (host JVM /actuator/prometheus)
item.description: Asserts the Prometheus text-exposition surface on the host log-monitoring-service JVM. The probe counter family is bootstrap-registered at P8.0 @PostConstruct (LD106) so it is guaranteed present on the very first scrape regardless of whether any probe has fired. The SLO gauges (cortex_monitoring_slo_budget_remaining + cortex_monitoring_slo_burn_rate) are NOT bootstrap-registered -- they appear lazily via MonitoringMetrics.recordSlo() once the SloEvaluator has produced its first SloSnapshot. The smoke script (smoke-p8-2a.ps1) waits for at least one evaluator tick (evaluation-interval=5s) before this collection runs, so by the time Newman fires the gauges are exposed.

item.name: GET /actuator/prometheus -> 200 + probe family + HELP+TYPE + Part 17 tag allowlist

exec: pm.test('probe family HELP+TYPE comments present', function () {
pm.test('probe series carries the backend=eureka-actuator tag (binder gate proven)', function () { pm.expect(text).to.match(/cortex_monitoring_probe_total\{[^}]*backend="eureka-actuator"/); });
pm.test('probe series carries the allowlisted outcome tag (Part 17)', function () { pm.expect(text).to.match(/cortex_monitoring_probe_total\{[^}]*outcome="/); });
pm.test('probe series carries the allowlisted service_id tag (Part 17)', function () { pm.expect(text).to.match(/cortex_monitoring_probe_total\{[^}]*service_id="/); });
// SLO gauges are bootstrap-registered by the P8.2a smoke pre-eval step OR by the SloEvaluator @Scheduled tick at 5s cadence.
pm.test('SLO budget remaining gauge family exposed', function () { pm.expect(text).to.include('cortex_monitoring_slo_budget_remaining'); });
pm.test('SLO burn rate gauge family exposed', function () { pm.expect(text).to.include('cortex_monitoring_slo_burn_rate'); });
pm.test('SLO budget gauge HELP+TYPE comments present', function () {
pm.test('SLO burn gauge HELP+TYPE comments present', function () {
pm.test('SLO budget series carries the service_id tag', function () { pm.expect(text).to.match(/cortex_monitoring_slo_budget_remaining\{[^}]*service_id="/); });
pm.test('SLO budget series carries the slo_name tag', function () { pm.expect(text).to.match(/cortex_monitoring_slo_budget_remaining\{[^}]*slo_name="/); });

item.name: Prometheus-Container (Optional -- skip if no prometheus_base_url)
item.description: Hits the Prometheus container on :9090 to assert: (a) the container is healthy; (b) the slo-burn-rate.rules.yml file parsed cleanly and Prometheus loaded the three alert rules (CortexSloFastBurn + CortexSloSlowBurn + CortexSloBudgetExhausted); (c) the scrape target for log-monitoring-service on host.docker.internal:8098 is reachable. Skips when prometheus_base_url is empty (staging / prod environments).

exec: const alertNames = allRules.filter(function (r) { return r.type === 'alerting'; }).map(function (r) { return r.name; });
pm.test('CortexSloFastBurn alert rule loaded (ADR-0046 D6)', function () { pm.expect(alertNames).to.include('CortexSloFastBurn'); });
pm.test('CortexSloSlowBurn alert rule loaded (ADR-0046 D6)', function () { pm.expect(alertNames).to.include('CortexSloSlowBurn'); });
pm.test('CortexSloBudgetExhausted alert rule loaded (ADR-0046 D6)', function () { pm.expect(alertNames).to.include('CortexSloBudgetExhausted'); });
const monitoringTarget = activeTargets.find(function (t) { return t.labels && t.labels.job === 'log-monitoring-service'; });
pm.test('log-monitoring-service scrape target is registered', function () { pm.expect(monitoringTarget, 'no scrape target with job=log-monitoring-service').to.not.be.undefined; });
pm.test('log-monitoring-service scrape target health == up', function () { pm.expect(monitoringTarget && monitoringTarget.health).to.equal('up'); });

item.name: Metrics-After
item.description: Re-snapshot the counter family AFTER the rest of the run. Asserts the family is still exported (no NaN, no missing series) and that the snapshot value is >= the baseline (counters never decrease). Does NOT assert exact delta -- Postman cannot guarantee no other caller incremented the counter on the shared smoke stack.

item.name: GET /actuator/prometheus after -> assert probe counter monotonic non-decreasing

exec: const re = new RegExp('^' + metricName + '\\{[^}]*backend="' + backend + '"[^}]*\\}\\s+([0-9.eE+-]+)$', 'gm');
pm.test('probe family still exported with HELP+TYPE', function () {
pm.test('SLO budget gauge family still exported', function () { pm.expect(text).to.include('cortex_monitoring_slo_budget_remaining'); });
pm.test('SLO burn gauge family still exported', function () { pm.expect(text).to.include('cortex_monitoring_slo_burn_rate'); });
